task_completion.description: The Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive application is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen displays the interface of the Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app.

task_completion.description: Dabrafenib is selected as the cancer drug.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Dabrafenib' is visible in a search bar, dropdown, or list of selected cancer drugs within the app.

task_completion.description: The Antifungal agents category is accessed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Antifungal agents' category is visible, expanded, or selected in the comedication menu.

task_completion.description: Ketoconazole is selected as the comedication.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Ketoconazole' is selected or added to the list of drugs to check alongside Dabrafenib.

task_completion.description: The interaction result between Dabrafenib and Ketoconazole is displayed on the screen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A results screen, popup, or panel is visible showing the specific drug-drug interaction details between Dabrafenib and Ketoconazole.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface of the app rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show standard GUI navigation (mouse clicks, typing in search boxes) rather than terminal windows executing scripts or API calls.

integrity.description: The interaction result is displayed within the actual Liverpool Cancer iChart app.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interaction details are shown in the app's native UI, not in a web browser (e.g., Google search), a text editor, or a generic document.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply type the expected answer into a text field to mimic completion.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interaction result appears as formatted app output (e.g., color-coded warnings, structured text) rather than raw text typed by the user.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Dabrafenib is a cancer drug (BRAF inhibitor) and Ketoconazole is an antifungal agent. The interaction between them is clinically significant due to potent CYP3A4 inhibition by ketoconazole, which increases dabrafenib AUC by 71% and Cmax by 33%. The VLM should look for an interaction result indicating a severe/red warning or mentioning CYP3A4 inhibition.
